graph.endBeforeStart: End date is before start date
graph.cannotDropEpicOnEpic: Epics cannot be nested inside other epics. Use dependencies to link them instead.
graph.cannotDropSprintOnSprint: Sprints cannot be placed inside other sprints. Use dependencies to order them.
graph.cannotDropSprintOnEpic: Sprints cannot be placed inside epics. Assign individual items to epics instead.
graph.invalidDrop: This drop action is not supported.
graph.cannotDependParentChild: A task cannot depend on its own parent story, or vice versa. Dependencies work between siblings or across stories.

authError.title: Login failed
authError.badState: The login session expired. Please try again.
authError.csrf: Security check failed. Please try again.
authError.exchangeFailed: Could not complete login with the provider. Please try again.
authError.dbError: A server error occurred. Please try again later.
authError.default: An unexpected error occurred during login.

myWork.title: My Work
myWork.noItemsHelp: Items appear here when you're added as a project team member and have stories or tasks assigned to you. Make sure your account email matches your team member email, or that you accepted an invitation.

orgs.title: Organisations
orgs.noOrgsYet: No organisations yet. Create one to get started.
orgs.confirmDelete: Permanently delete this organisation and all its data?

orgDetail.noContactDetails: No contact details yet. Click "Edit Details" to add them.
orgDetail.noProjectsYet: No projects yet. Create one to get started.

invite.invalidLink: This invitation link is not valid.
invite.alreadyAcceptedMessage: This invitation has already been used. You can log in with your credentials.
invite.expiredMessage: This invitation has expired. Please ask your team to send a new one.
invite.invitedToJoin: You've been invited to join a project
invite.passwordTooShort: Password must be at least 8 characters

board.helpBody1: This is your team's work visualised as a flow. Each column represents a stage in your workflow.
board.helpBody2: Drag cards between columns to update their status instantly. When you move a story, its child tasks move with it.
board.helpBody3: Keep the "In Progress" column small; limiting work-in-progress helps your team finish things instead of starting new ones.

backlog.helpBody1: The backlog is your prioritised list of work. Items at the top are highest priority.
backlog.helpBody2: Drag items by the grip handle to reprioritise. Use the filters to focus on stories, bugs, or specific statuses.
backlog.helpBody3: Click + New Item to quickly add stories or tasks.
backlog.noItemsMatch: No items match your filters. Try adjusting or create a new item.

sprints.helpBody1: Sprints are time-boxed iterations (typically 2 weeks). Pull stories from the backlog into a sprint to plan your team's work.
sprints.helpBody2: Watch the capacity indicator to avoid overloading the team.
sprints.title: Sprints
sprints.noSprintsYet: No sprints yet. Create one to start planning.

workItemDetail.descPlaceholder: Describe the requirement, goal, and context...
workItemDetail.storyPointsHelp: For stories, points are the sum of all task points. Add points to individual tasks during refinement.
workItemDetail.taskPointsHelp: Estimate the relative effort. Common scales: 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 13. A "1" is the simplest thing your team does.
workItemDetail.pointsUsedHelp: Actual effort spent. Defaults to the estimate when marked done. Adjust if the task took more or less effort than estimated.
workItemDetail.largeStoryWarning: This story is {{points}} points. Consider breaking it into smaller stories that can each be completed in a single sprint.
workItemDetail.convertToStoryConfirm: Convert this task to a story? It will maintain a dependency link to the original story.
workItemDetail.convertToStoryHelp: Promotes this task to an independent story with a dependency link

childTasks.title: Tasks ({{count}})
childTasks.contextHelp: Tasks break a story into work by discipline, such as UX design, backend dev, frontend dev and QE testing. Each task is assignable to a team member.

acceptanceCriteria.title: Acceptance Criteria
acceptanceCriteria.contextHelp: Acceptance Criteria define when this item is truly "done." Written in Given / When / Then format (BDD), they make expectations explicit and testable. Each criterion is a specific, verifiable condition, not a vague wish.
acceptanceCriteria.noCriteriaYet: No acceptance criteria yet. Define the conditions for "done".

dependencies.title: Dependencies
dependencies.contextHelp: Dependencies track relationships between items. "Depends on" means this item can't proceed until the target is complete. "Relates to" is an informational link.

links.title: Links
links.labelPlaceholder: Label (e.g. GitHub PR, Figma, Test results, Docs)

testResults.retestMessage: This defect has {{count}} failing test(s). Upload new test results (JUnit XML or webhook) after the fix is deployed to clear the QE gate and close this item.
testResults.title: Test Results

createEpic.title: Create Epic
createEpic.descriptionPlaceholder: Business goal and scope of this epic...

team.title: Team
team.noMembersYet: No team members yet. Add people to start assigning work.

epics.helpBody1: Epics are feature-level groupings. Each epic contains related stories that together deliver a business capability.
epics.helpBody2: Click an epic to see its stories, add new ones, and track progress.
epics.title: Epics
epics.noEpicsYet: No epics yet. Epics group related stories into feature-level deliverables.

epicDetail.descPlaceholder: Describe the business goal and scope...
epicDetail.storiesContextHelp: Stories are the deliverable goals of this epic. Each story represents a specific user-facing outcome that can be completed within a single sprint.
epicDetail.noStoriesYet: No stories in this epic yet. Add one to define the goals.
epicDetail.titlePlaceholder: As a [role], I want [goal], so that [benefit]
epicDetail.autoTaskHint: A task will be auto-created for the assignee with {{points}} point(s). Points always live on tasks.
epicDetail.largeStoryWarning: Large story ({{points}} points). Consider breaking this into smaller stories that can each be completed in a single sprint. Smaller stories are easier to estimate, test, and deliver.
epicDetail.acContextHelp: Define when this story is "done." Each criterion should be specific and testable.
epicDetail.typeToSearch: Showing first 20 of {{count}} — type to search

sprintDetail.allStoriesAssigned: All stories are already in a sprint or completed.
sprintDetail.noItemsYet: No items in this sprint yet. Click "+ Add Stories" to pull from the backlog.
sprintDetail.removeStoryAndTasks: Remove story and tasks from sprint

reports.helpBody1: Reports give you visibility into your team's delivery. Velocity shows how many points you complete per sprint; it stabilises over time.
reports.helpBody2: Use the sprint progress and team workload charts to spot bottlenecks early.
reports.title: Reports
reports.noCompletedSprints: No completed sprints yet. Velocity will appear after your first sprint is completed.
reports.noWorkload: No items assigned in the active sprint.
reports.noBurndownData: No burndown data yet. Data appears as items move through statuses during the sprint.

projectDetails.title: Project Details
projectDetails.name: Project Name
projectDetails.descPlaceholder: Describe the project goals and scope...

settings.projectSettingsDesc: Set the default time horizons for this project. These auto-fill dates when creating epics and sprints, and inform timeline planning.
settings.projectDurationHelp: Overall delivery timeline for this project.
settings.epicDurationHelp: Auto-fills epic due dates from start date.
settings.sprintDurationHelp: Auto-fills sprint end dates from start date.

aiSettings.title: AI Configuration
aiSettings.description: Configure an AI provider to enable acceptance criteria suggestions, story decomposition, and release note drafting. You provide your own API key; PlanA doesn't store or proxy through any shared account.
aiSettings.apiKeyHelp: Your key is stored encrypted per-project. Leave blank to keep the current key.
aiSettings.sharingDescription: Create shareable links for stakeholders to view a read-only project dashboard. No login required; the link is the access.
aiSettings.label: Label

reportView.title: Reports
reportView.generateDescription: Generate a comprehensive report from your project data.
